commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow repair can preserve the useful leak fix while addressing the output-flush blocker before merge.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete hook-enforcement risk because forced process exit can cut off denial output before Codex consumes it.; Review finding: [P1] Flush hook output before forcing exit — src/cli/hooks-cli.ts:563 (sha=62b98bdeedb6565b519518e7af1f4d85c329f610)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a stdin timeout, a hard native hook relay process deadline, forced relay process exit, and focused relay timeout tests.

PR surface: Source +62, Tests +43. Total +105 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main has an unbounded stdin read and only sets process.exitCode, and the PR body supplies a terminal MRE showing leaked open-stdin relays before the patch; I did not run a live MRE in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow repair can preserve the useful leak fix while addressing the output-flush blocker before merge.

target.title: fix(cli): bound native hook relay process lifetime (#90993)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90993 by bounding native hook relay CLI (`openclaw-hooks`) process lifetime so leaked subprocesses cannot accumulate when harnesses leave stdin open or gateway handles remain referenced.


## Root cause

Three stacked issues in the relay CLI path:

1. **Unbounded stdin read** — `readStreamText()` blocked forever on open stdin before `--timeout` applied.
2. **No hard exit** — relay action set `process.exitCode` only; Node stayed alive while stdin/WebSocket handles remained open.
3. **Narrow timeout scope** — `--timeout` bounded only bridge/gateway RPC, not stdin or overall process lifetime.

## Changes

- `src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.ts`: time-bound stdin read; export `resolveNativeHookRelayProcessDeadlineMs()`.
- `src/cli/hooks-cli.ts`: arm hard process deadline (`process.exit(124)` on overrun); call `process.exit(exitCode)` after relay work.

## Real behavior proof

### Repro (before fix, on main @ e06f6ffc / v2026.6.1)

```bash
for i in $(seq 1 10); do
  tail -f /dev/null | node scripts/run-node.mjs hooks relay \
    --provider codex --relay-id "mre-test-$i" --generation "mre-gen-$i" \
    --event pre_tool_use --timeout 5000 &
  sleep 2
done
sleep 8
ps -eo pid,comm,rss | grep openclaw-hooks
```

**Before:** 10 `openclaw-hooks` processes, ~250–450 MB RSS each (~2.5 GB total), alive past `--timeout 5000`.

### Verification (after fix, this branch)

Same commands after `pnpm build`:

```
process count: 0
(no comm=openclaw-hooks)
```

Open-stdin relay exits within stdin timeout; no `openclaw-hooks` zombies after 8s.

```bash
pnpm test src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.test.ts
# 17 passed
```

## Tests

- `pnpm test src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.test.ts` — 17 passed (includes new stdin-timeout + deadline helper tests)
- `pnpm build` — passed locally

## Release note (for maintainers; no CHANGELOG edit per CONTRIBUTING)

CLI: bound native hook relay stdin read and force process exit so `openclaw-hooks` subprocesses cannot leak after hook events. (#90993)

## Sign-off

- Models used: AI-assisted
- Submitter effort: implemented fix + tests + local MRE verification
- Not tested: multi-hour gateway/heartbeat soak (MRE models the same open-stdin failure mode)


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
target.author: vansh5632
